Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hmm, this is a process modeling task with clear requirements. The user wants me to fix an underfitting process tree that currently allows too much parallelism in a procure-to-pay process.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to first analyze why the current model is problematic. The parallel block lets steps like Receive Goods and Pay Invoice happen interchangeably, which doesnt make sense in real procurement workflows.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The correct sequence should be strictly linear after approval until closing.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the standard process, I can see all steps should be sequential except possibly some optional steps, but the prompt doesnt mention any exceptions. The fixed model should maintain all 8 activities in exact order with no parallelism.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Ill create a simple SEQUENCE tree that mirrors the 1-8 numbering exactly. The explanation should highlight how this eliminates the dangerous flexibility of the original model while keeping the same components. The key is emphasizing that procurement requires strict ordering for control and audit purposes.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The response structure seems clear: brief issue analysis first, then the corrected model code, followed by explanation of why this works better. No need for complex operators since the requirement is just to enforce the standard order.
